properties.remark: TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED SOME MINOR FLOODING OF STREETS IN AND AROUND LEBANON.
SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE TO AT LEAST 1 HOME NEAR KEYSBURG ROAD AND HUGH GILL ROAD FROM POSSIBLE TORNADO.
A HOUSE WAS DESTROYED AND OTHER DAMAGE OCCURRED FROM A POSSIBLE TORNADO NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF KEYSBURG ROAD... HUGH GILL ROAD... AND MCGEE ROAD.
A HOUSE WAS DESTROYED AND OTHER DAMAGE OCCURRED FROM A POSSIBLE TTORNADO NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF KEYSBURG ROAD... HUGH GILL ROAD... AND MCGEE ROAD.

AN EF-1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN A HALF MILE SOUTH OF THE KY/TN STATE LINE BETWEEN HUGH GILL ROAD AND KEYSBURG ROAD AND MOVED NORTHEAST WITH NUMEROUS TREES SNAPPED OR UPROOTED. THE ROOF OF A BARN WAS BLOWN OFF ON HUGH GILL ROAD. A HOME SUFFERED SIGNIFICANT ROOF AND EXTERIOR DAMAGE ON MCGHEE ROAD AND A NEARBY SMALL BARN WAS DESTROYED. THE TORNADO THEN MOVED INTO KENTUCKY AND INTENSIFIED TO EF-2. PATH LENGTH IN TN 0.45 MILES. PATH WIDTH IN TN 150 YARDS. MAX WINDS IN TN 105 MPH.
TREE DOWN AT 7600 GREENBRIER CREEK ROAD IN FAR NORTHWEST HUMPHREYS COUNTY
TREE DOWN ON HIGHWAY 147
PORCH BLOWN OFF HOME
TREE DOWN ON HIGHWAY 233
TREE DOWN ON HIGHWAY 13

AN EF-1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR THE DOTSONVILLE COMMUNITY AND MOVED NORTHEAST ENDING ALONG THE CUMBERLAND RIVER JUST WEST OF CLARKSVILLE. A FEW HOMES SUFFERED ROOF DAMAGE ON GIP MANNING ROAD AND HUNTING CREEK ROAD... AND UP TO 15 HOMES WERE MORE SEVERELY DAMAGED ON MARTIN ROAD... DEEPWOOD DRIVE... AND DEEPWOOD TRAIL. HUNDREDS OF TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN ALONG THE PATH INCLUDING ON BRADLEY A. MARTIN ROAD AND RUSTYS LANE. MAX WINDS 105 MPH. PATH LENGTH 5.04 MILES. PATH WIDTH 150 YARDS.
SOME HOMES DAMAGED AND MINOR INJURIES REPORTED IN THE CLARKSVILLE AREA.

GROCERY STORE WINDOWS BLOWN OUT AND ROOF DAMAGE AT AUSTIN PEAY STATE UNIVERSITY IN CLARKSVILLE. AUSTIN PEAY/MURRAY STATE GAME IN CLARKSVILLE DELAYED BY SOME MINOR ROOF DAMAGE AND WATER LEAKING ONTO THE ARENA FLOOR.
LARGE TREE UPROOTED ON DUNBAR CAVE ROAD.

*** 2 INJ *** AN EF2 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN EAST CLARKSVILLE ON DUNBAR CAVE RD THEN MOVED ENE ACROSS I-24 BEFORE ENDING ON KIRKWOOD RD. TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN N OF DUNBAR CAVE RD AND OUTBUILDINGS DAMAGED AT ROSSVIEW HIGH SCHOOL. THREE BUILDINGS AT AN INDUSTRIAL COMPLEX E OF I-24 AND 75 VEHICLES IN THE PARKING LOTS WERE DAMAGED. DOZENS OF HOMES WERE DAMAGED OR DESTROYED ON ROLLOW LANE AND IN THE FARMINGTON SUBDIVISION WITH 2 INJURIES. PATH LENGTH 4.05 MILES AND PATH WIDTH 300 YARDS. MAX WINDS 125 MPH.
*** 2 INJ *** SIGNIFICANT HOME DAMAGE IN THE FARMINGTON SUBDIVISION EAST OF CLARKSVILLE. HEAVY DAMAGE TO A CHURCH. AT LEAST 2 INJURED.

AN EF1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN EAST OF ADAMS NEAR JOHNSON SPRINGS ROAD AND MOVED NORTHEAST BEFORE ENDING ALONG BUZZARD CREEK ROAD. ONE HOME SUFFERED MINOR ROOF DAMAGE ON STURGEON CREEK ROAD AND DOZENS OF TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED ALONG BOTH STURGEON CREEK ROAD AND BARNES ROAD. SEVERAL BARNS AND OUTBUILDINGS WERE DAMAGED OR DESTROYED ALONG THE BEND OF BUZZARD CREEK ROAD. PATH LENGTH 3.13 MILES. PATH WIDTH 150 YARDS. MAX WINDS 95 MPH.
SEVERAL BARNS HAD SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE NEAR 5700 BUZZARD CREEK ROAD.

RECEIVED A CALL FROM THE PUBLIC THAT HAD SIGNIFICANT ROOF DAMAGE ON ALL SIDES OF HOUSE FROM THE STORM YESTERDAY AFTERNOON/EVENING. THE CALLER SAID THE TIME WAS BETWEEN 5 AND 5:30 PM. LOOKING AT RADAR IT LOOKS LIKE THE TIME SHOULD BE CLOSER TO 4:15 PM. CALLER ALSO SAID THERE WAS AT LEAST ONE TREE DOWN IN HER NEIGHBORHOOD.

A SMALL BRIEF EF-0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ON THE WEST SIDE OF BRADYVILLE ROAD JUST NORTH OF LASSITER ROAD. MOVING NNE THE TORNADO CAUSED SIGNIFICANT ROOF DAMAGE TO TWO HOMES ON THE WEST SIDE OF THE ROADWAY. FURTHER NNE AN OLD BARN WAS DESTROYED WHERE THREE MEN ESCAPED INJURY AND ANOTHER HOME BUILT IN THE 1890S SUSTAINED MINOR ROOF DAMAGE WITH SEVERAL TREES BLOWN DOWN. SEVERAL EYEWITNESS ACCOUNTS STATE THIS TORNADO SKIPPED ALONG ITS PATH AND WAS ONLY ON THE GROUND 1-2 MINUTES.

THIS IS A CONTINUATION OF A TORNADO THAT STARTED IN LAUDERDALE COUNTY, ALABAMA. THE EF0 TORNADO CROSSED INTO TENNESSEE SNAPPING SEVERAL LARGE BRANCHES AND CAUSING MINOR ROOF DAMAGE TO A HOME ON MAY BRANCH LOOP. AS THE TORNADO MOVED NORTHEAST, IT UPROOTED MANY TREES AND ENDED UP DESTROYING AN OUTBUILDING AND CAUSING MINOR ROOF DAMAGE TO A HOME ON MIDDLE CYPRESS CREEK ROAD. FINALLY, IT CROSSED GEORGE OLIVE ROAD, DID SOME MINOR SHINGLE DAMAGE TO A HOME BEFORE LIFTING.

THE EF1 TORNADO BEGAN ON BLUFF ROAD IN SOUTHERN LAWRENCE COUNTY. AS IT MOVED NORTHEAST IT ROLLED A MOBILE HOME, DEMOLISHED 2 HAY BARNS AND CAUSED MINOR ROOF DAMAGE TO 2 HOMES. IN LORETTO THE TORNADO CAUSED EXTENSIVE DAMAGE TO THE CITY'S SPORTS COMPLEX...DESTROYING 3 CINDER BLOCK DUGOUTS AND TOSSING 2 LARGE SECTIONS OF BLEACHERS. *** 1 FATAL, 3 INJ *** THE EF-2 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN NORTHERN BEDFORD COUNTY AND DAMAGED A FEW OUTBUILDINGS AND TREES. IT QUICKLY INCREASED IN INTENSITY TO EF-2 AS IT MOVED INTO RUTHERFORD COUNTY WHERE IT DAMAGED/DESTROYED 2 HOUSES ON KINGDOM RD. IT TOOK THE ROOF OFF A WELL BUILD BRICK HOME, THEN MOVED NORTHEAST AND FLIPPED A HOME OFF OF ITS FOUNDATION, KILLING ONE PERSON. ANOTHER HOME LOST ITS ROOF AND 3RD FLOOR. BEFORE LIFTING THE TORNADO COMPLETELY DESTROYED TWO SILOS AND STRUCK A MOBILE HOME ON ROCK SPRINGS RD.
HOUSE COMPLETELY COLLAPSED AT 8085 MIDLAND ROAD IN CHRISTIANA...POSSIBLE TORNADO
REPORTS OF SEVERAL STRUCTURES DAMAGED, INCLUDING A ROOF MISSING FROM A STRUCTURE ON ROCK SPRINGS ROAD. POSSIBLE TORNADO.

THE EF-1 TORNADO BEGAN NEAR AUBURNTOWN IN CANNON COUNTY ON HURRICANE CREEK RD. IT CAUSED SPORADIC TREE DAMAGE AS IT CONTINUED NORTHEAST. A CARPORT AND WELL BUILT TAXIDERMY BARN WERE COMPLETELY DESTROYED OFF OF SYCAMORE CREEK RD. IT CAUSED ADDITIONAL TREE DAMAGE IN DEKALB COUNTY AND DAMAGED THE ROOF AND SIDING OF A HOUSE ON CLEAR FORK RD BEFORE LIFTING NEAR DRY CREEK ROAD.
TREE LIMBS AND POWER LINES DOWN. RESPONDERS ON SCENE. POSSIBLE TORNADO DAMAGE.

A CONTINUATION OF THE EF-2 TORNADO IN FRANKLIN CO, TN. THE TORNADO ENTERED COFFEE CO. AS AN EF-0 TORNADO, SNAPPING LARGE BRANCHES AND CAUSING ROOF DAMAGE TO A BARN. IT DESTROYED SEVERAL OUTBUILDINGS AND TORE THE FRONT PORCH OFF OF A HOUSE. THE TORNADO INTENSIFIED TO AN EF-2 IN GRUNDY CO WHERE IT DAMAGED SOME HOMES ON CONRY CIRCLE AND COMPLETELY RIPPED THE ROOF OFF A BRICK HOME ON HWY 50. IT CONTINUED THROUGH GRUNDY KNOCKING DOWN POSSIBLY 1500 TO 2000 TREES AND LIFTED JUST BEFORE SEQUATCHI CO.

A CONTINUATION OF THE EF-2 TORNADO IN FRANKLIN CO, TN. THE TORNADO ENTERED COFFEE CO. AS AN EF-0 TORNADO, SNAPPING LARGE BRANCHES AND CAUSING ROOF DAMAGE TO A BARN. IT DESTROYED SEVERAL OUTBUILDINGS AND TORE THE FRONT PORCH OFF OF A HOUSE. THE TORNADO INTENSIFIED TO AN EF-2 IN GRUNDY CO WHERE IT DAMAGED SOME HOMES ON CONRY CIRCLE AND COMPLETELY RIPPED THE ROOF OFF A BRICK HOME ON HWY 50. IT CONTINUED THROUGH GRUNDY KNOCKING DOWN POSSIBLY 1500 TO 2000 TREES AND LIFTED JUST BEFORE SEQUATCHIE CO.

THE EF0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ON JOSH BROWN ROAD AND BETTY LANE WHERE IT SNAPPED TREES AND BROKE SEVERAL LARGE BRANCHES. AS IT MOVED EAST IT DESTROYED A BARN ON SOUTH BUNKER HILL ROAD AND OVERTURNED CONTAINERS AND A TRAILER AT THE UPPER CUMBERLAND REGIONAL AIRPORT. ACROSS HWY 111 THERE WAS DAMAGE TO AN OUTBUILDING'S METAL ROOF AND LARGE BRANCHES BROKEN. THE PATH CONCLUDED ON POST OAK BRIDGE ROAD WHERE A TREE FELL ONTO A BARN AND LARGE BRANCHES WERE SNAPPED OFF TREES.

THE EF0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ON JOSH BROWN ROAD AND BETTY LANE WHERE IT SNAPPED TREES AND BROKE SEVERAL LARGE BRANCHES. AS IT MOVED EAST IT DESTROYED A BARN ON SOUTH BUNKER HILL ROAD AND OVERTURNED CONTAINERS AND A TRAILER AT THE UPPER CUMBERLAND REGIONAL AIRPORT. ACROSS HWY 111 THERE WAS DAMAGE TO AN OUTBUILDING METAL ROOF AND LARGE BRANCHES BROKEN. THE PATH CONCLUDED ON POST OAK BRIDGE ROAD WHERE A TREE FELL ONTO A BARN AND LARGE BRANCHES WERE SNAPPED OFF TREES.
MULTI-VEHICLE ACCIDENT INCLUDING A TRACTOR TRAILER THAT FLIPPED AT MM126. A SECOND TRACTOR TRAILER ALSO HIT ANOTHER TRUCK. DOWNED TREES REPORTED. POSSIBLE TORNADO.
